subtopics: Biodiversity and Need for classification
Three domains of life, taxonomy and systematics
Concept of species and taxonomical hierarchy
Binomial nomenclature
key_concepts: Levels of biological organisation from molecules to organisms
Interactions at organismal level up to ecosystems and biomes

subtopics: Five kingdom classification system
Salient features of Monera, Protista and Fungi
Classification into major groups: Lichens, Viruses and Viroids
key_concepts: Patterns of inheritance in terms of Mendel's laws at population level
Evidences of biological evolution and natural selection consequences

subtopics: Classification of plants into major groups: Algae, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnospermae and Angiosperms
key_concepts: Salient and distinguishing features with examples for each group
Changes in allele frequency structure and function due to evolution

subtopics: Classification of non-chordates up to phyla level and chordates upto class level
key_concepts: Salient features with examples for each category
Linkages between scientific knowledge across other curricular areas (e.g., sound pitch, muscle use in dance)

units.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

subtopics: Morphology of root, stem, leaf, inflorescence, flower, fruit and seed
key_concepts: Description of family Solanaceae

subtopics: Tissue systems in dicots and monocots
key_concepts: Anatomy and functions of tissue systems

subtopics: Morphology, Anatomy and functions of digestive system
key_concepts: Circulatory, respiratory, nervous and reproductive systems in frog

subtopics: Cell theory and cell as basic unit
key_concepts: Structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells (Plant/Animal)
Cell envelope, membrane, wall
Organelles: ER, Golgi, Lysosomes, Vacuoles, Mitochondria, Ribosomes, Plastids, Microbodies
Cytoskeleton, cilia, flagella, centrioles (ultrastructure and function)
Nucleus

subtopics: Chemical constituents: Proteins, Carbohydrates, Lipids, Nucleic acids
key_concepts: Structure and function of biomolecules
Enzyme types, properties and action

subtopics: Cell cycle phases
key_concepts: Mitosis and Meiosis processes
Significance of cell division

subtopics: Site of photosynthesis and pigments
key_concepts: Photochemical and biosynthetic phases
Cyclic and non-cyclic photophosphorylation
Chemiosmotic hypothesis, Photorespiration
C3 and C4 pathways
Factors affecting photosynthesis

subtopics: Exchange of gases and cellular respiration pathways
key_concepts: Glycolysis, Fermentation (anaerobic), TCA cycle, Electron transport system (aerobic)
Energy relations: ATP generation
Amphibolic pathways and Respiratory quotient

subtopics: Seed germination, phases of growth and rate
key_concepts: Conditions of growth: differentiation, dedifferentiation, redifferentiation
Sequence of developmental processes in plant cells
Plant growth regulators (Auxin, Gibberellin, Cytokinin, Ethylene, ABA)

subtopics: Flower structure and development of gametophytes
Pollination types, agencies, out-breeding devices, pollen-pistil interaction
Double fertilization and post-fertilization events (endosperm/embryo)
Development of seed and fruit formation
Special modes: apomixis, parthenocarpy, polyembryony
key_concepts: Significance of seed dispersal and fruit formation

subtopics: Male and female reproductive systems; microscopic anatomy of testis and ovary
Gametogenesis: spermatogenesis and oogenesis
Menstrual cycle, fertilization, embryo development to blastocyst
Implantation, pregnancy, placenta formation (elementary)
Parturition and lactation (elementary)
key_concepts: Elementary ideas of parturition and lactation

subtopics: Need for reproductive health and prevention of STDs
Birth control: need, methods, contraception
Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP) and amniocentesis
Infertility and Assisted Reproductive Technologies (IVF, ZIFT, GIFT)
General awareness on reproductive health issues
key_concepts: Assisted reproductive technologies for general awareness

subtopics: Mendelian inheritance and deviations (incomplete dominance, co-dominance)
Multiple alleles, blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance
Chromosome theory of inheritance; sex determination mechanisms
Linkage, crossing over, sex-linked inheritance (haemophilia, colour blindness)
Mendelian and chromosomal disorders in humans
key_concepts: Down's syndrome, Turner's, Klinefelter's syndromes; thalassemia

subtopics: Search for genetic material; DNA and RNA structure
DNA packaging, replication, Central Dogma (transcription/translation)
Genetic code and gene expression regulation (lac operon)
Human genome project and rice genome projects
DNA fingerprinting
key_concepts: Central dogma and genetic code application

subtopics: Origin of life and evidences for evolution (paleontology, anatomy, embryology)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory
Mechanism of evolution: variation via mutation and recombination
